claim: FBI IC3 2024 reported a record $16.6 billion in losses across 859,532 complaints, a 33% jump over 2023.
quote: complaints totaled 859,532, with losses of $16.6 billion, representing a 33 percent increase from 2023

claim: Cyber-enabled fraud was 38% of IC3 2024 complaints but 83% of losses ($13.7B).
quote: cyber-enabled fraud accounted for 38 percent of all complaints received in 2024 and was responsible for a staggering 83 percent of the total financial losses reported

claim: BEC losses in 2024 were $2.77B across 21,442 incidents; investment fraud was the costliest category.
quote: In 2024 alone, BEC losses totaled $2.77 billion across 21,442 reported incidents

claim: Investment fraud topped IC3 2024 categories at roughly $6.57B; crypto-related complaints reached 149,686 with $9.3B losses (+66% YoY).
quote: in 2024, IC3 logged 149,686 cryptocurrency-related complaints, with total reported costs exceeding $9.3 billion—a 66% increase over 2023

claim: Elder fraud (60+) reported $4.9B in losses in 2024, up 43% YoY.
quote: Elder fraud across all scam types rose to $4.9 billion in reported losses in 2024—a 43% increase year-over-year

claim: FTC consumers reported $12.5B lost to fraud in 2024, up 25% YoY; investment scams led at $5.7B (+24%); imposter scams second at $2.95B.
quote: Consumers reported losing more than $12.5 billion to fraud last year, representing a 25% increase over the previous year. Investment scams accounted for the most fraud losses at $5.7 billion

claim: Government-imposter scam losses alone climbed $171M in 2024 to $789M, the leading FTC reported scam category.
quote: losses to government imposter scams in particular increased $171 million from 2023 to a total of $789 million in 2024

claim: Deloitte Center for Financial Services projects gen-AI-enabled US fraud losses growing from $12.3B (2023) to $40B by 2027 — 32% CAGR.
quote: gen AI could enable fraud losses to reach US$40 billion in the United States by 2027, from US$12.3 billion in 2023, a compound annual growth rate of 32%

claim: Sumsub 2025-2026 report: sophisticated multi-step fraud rose 180% YoY; multi-step share grew from 10% (2024) to 28% (2025).
quote: +180% YoY growth in 'sophisticated' fraud: the share of multi-step attacks rose from 10% (2024) to 28% (2025)

claim: Sumsub measured global identity fraud at 2.2% of verifications in 2025 (vs 2.6% in 2024); Maldives saw a 2,100% YoY jump in deepfake attacks.
quote: identity fraud attempts at 2.2% of all analyzed verifications worldwide – compared to 2.6% in 2024 ... suffered a 2100% year-on-year increase in deepfake attacks

claim: Chainalysis: 2025 crypto scam losses hit $17B; AI-enabled scams generated 4.5x more revenue per operation; impersonation scams grew 1,400% YoY; average scam payment rose 253% to $2,764.
quote: crypto scams generated over $17 billion in losses for 2025 ... Impersonation scams grew more than 1,400% year over year, driven in part by AI tools ... AI-enabled scams generated 4.5 times more revenue per operation than traditional scams

claim: Chainalysis 2024 data: pig-butchering / romance-baiting revenue grew ~40% YoY and deposits to those scams jumped ~210%.
quote: the number of deposits to pig butchering scammers increased 210% YoY in 2024

claim: UK Finance H1 2025: APP fraud losses £257.5M (+12% YoY); investment-scam losses £97.7M (+55%); romance scam losses +35%.
quote: APP fraud losses were £257.5 million in the first half of 2025, representing a 12% increase ... Investment scams led the surge, accounting for £97.7 million in losses — a 55% increase

claim: TRM Labs (Congressional testimony): FinCEN-designated Cambodian Huione Group received $39.6B in transaction volume; Chinese-language laundering networks processed $103B in 2025 (up from $123M in 2020).
quote: FinCEN's designation of Huione Group (a Cambodia-based network) as a primary money laundering concern in 2025 identified that organization as receiving USD 39.6 billion in transaction volume
